info.name: API Aggregator
info.description: Postman collection for the API Aggregator assignment. Tests authentication, aggregation across GitHub, HackerNews, and OpenMeteo, and request statistics, via both REST and GraphQL. REST is the primary, assignment-compliant interface (Swagger at http://localhost:5025/swagger); GraphQL (Hot Chocolate Nitro at https://localhost:7209/graphql/) is an additional interface that calls the same MediatR handlers. Built on Clean Architecture, MediatR, EF Core with PostgreSQL, and an in-memory thread-safe statistics store.

item.name: 00 - Authentication
item.description: Handles user authentication via both REST and GraphQL. Authenticates a demo user against configured credentials and issues a JWT used to authorize subsequent REST and GraphQL operations. Both surfaces call LoginCommand / LoginCommandHandler.

item.name: Login (REST)
item.description:
Authenticates a user via the REST endpoint and stores the returned JWT in the `accessToken` environment variable. This token is required for all protected REST and GraphQL operations.

Endpoint: `POST /api/auth/login`
Controller: `AuthController`
MediatR command: `LoginCommand` / `LoginCommandHandler`

exec: pm.test("Login response contains an access token", function () {

item.name: Authenticate User (GraphQL)
item.description:
Authenticates a user via the GraphQL `login` mutation and stores the returned JWT in the `accessToken` environment variable. This token is required for all protected REST and GraphQL operations.

GraphQL field: `login`
MediatR command: `LoginCommand` / `LoginCommandHandler`

exec: pm.test("Login response contains a token", function () {

item.name: Validate Authorized Request
item.description:
Confirms that the stored JWT is valid and can access a protected GraphQL operation by calling Get API Statistics.

GraphQL field: `apiStatistics`

item.name: 01 - Aggregation
item.description: Retrieves unified aggregated data from GitHub, HackerNews, and Open-Meteo via both REST and GraphQL. Both surfaces call AggregateDataQuery / AggregateDataQueryHandler, which executes providers in parallel, applies filtering and sorting, and records in-memory statistics. All requests in this folder require a valid JWT.

item.name: Get Aggregated Data (REST)
item.description:
Runs the aggregation query via REST against all enabled providers with no filters applied. Returns unified items and per-provider status (success, fallback usage, response time, error message).

Endpoint: `GET /api/aggregation`
Controller: `AggregationController`
MediatR query: `AggregateDataQuery` / `AggregateDataQueryHandler`

item.name: Get Aggregated Data by Keyword (REST)
item.description:
Runs the aggregation query via REST filtered by keyword (e.g. 'dotnet'). The keyword is applied to GitHub repository search and HackerNews story title filtering, and as an in-memory filter on the combined results.

Endpoint: `GET /api/aggregation`
Controller: `AggregationController`
MediatR query: `AggregateDataQuery` / `AggregateDataQueryHandler`

item.name: Get Aggregated Data Sorted by Relevance (REST)
item.description:
Runs the aggregation query via REST with results sorted by relevance score in descending order.

Endpoint: `GET /api/aggregation`
Controller: `AggregationController`
MediatR query: `AggregateDataQuery` / `AggregateDataQueryHandler`

item.name: Get Aggregated Data by Provider (REST)
item.description:
Runs the aggregation query via REST restricted to a single provider (e.g. GitHub only) using repeated `providers` query parameters.

Endpoint: `GET /api/aggregation`
Controller: `AggregationController`
MediatR query: `AggregateDataQuery` / `AggregateDataQueryHandler`

item.name: Aggregate Data (GraphQL)
item.description:
Runs the aggregation query via GraphQL against all enabled providers with no filters applied. Returns unified items and per-provider status (success, fallback usage, response time, error message).

GraphQL field: `aggregateData`
MediatR query: `AggregateDataQuery` / `AggregateDataQueryHandler`

exec: pm.test("Aggregate data returns items array", function () {
pm.test("Aggregate data returns provider statuses", function () {

item.name: Aggregate Data by Keyword (GraphQL)
item.description:
Runs the aggregation query via GraphQL filtered by keyword (e.g. 'dotnet'). The keyword is applied to GitHub repository search and HackerNews story title filtering, and as an in-memory filter on the combined results.

GraphQL field: `aggregateData`
MediatR query: `AggregateDataQuery` / `AggregateDataQueryHandler`

exec: pm.test("Aggregate data returns items array", function () {
pm.test("Aggregate data returns provider statuses", function () {

item.name: Aggregate Data Sorted by Relevance (GraphQL)
item.description:
Runs the aggregation query via GraphQL with results sorted by relevance score in descending order.

GraphQL field: `aggregateData`
MediatR query: `AggregateDataQuery` / `AggregateDataQueryHandler`

exec: pm.test("Aggregate data returns items array", function () {

item.name: Aggregate Data by Provider (GraphQL)
item.description:
Runs the aggregation query via GraphQL restricted to a single provider (e.g. GitHub only) using the `providers` filter.

GraphQL field: `aggregateData`
MediatR query: `AggregateDataQuery` / `AggregateDataQueryHandler`

item.name: 02 - Providers
item.description: Not implemented. Reserved for future provider management endpoints (e.g. Get Providers, Get Provider Health, Refresh Provider). The current solution registers providers via IExternalApiClient and DI; provider configuration is not exposed through the API.

item.name: 03 - Statistics
item.description: Retrieves per-provider request statistics and performance buckets via both REST and GraphQL, sourced from the thread-safe in-memory statistics store (InMemoryApiStatisticsStore). Both surfaces call GetApiStatisticsQuery / GetApiStatisticsQueryHandler. This endpoint does not depend on PostgreSQL.

item.name: Get API Statistics (REST)
item.description:
Returns request statistics grouped by provider via REST: total/successful/failed request counts, average response time, and performance buckets (fast < 100ms, average 100-200ms, slow > 200ms).

Endpoint: `GET /api/statistics`
Controller: `StatisticsController`
MediatR query: `GetApiStatisticsQuery` / `GetApiStatisticsQueryHandler`

item.name: Get API Statistics (GraphQL)
item.description:
Returns request statistics grouped by provider via GraphQL: total/successful/failed request counts, average response time, and performance buckets (fast < 100ms, average 100-200ms, slow > 200ms).

GraphQL field: `apiStatistics`
MediatR query: `GetApiStatisticsQuery` / `GetApiStatisticsQueryHandler`

item.name: 04 - Health & Diagnostics
item.description: Not implemented. Reserved for a future REST health check endpoint (e.g. GET /health) and GraphQL schema introspection examples.

item.name: 99 - Examples / Edge Cases
item.description: Demonstrates validation, authorization, and error handling behavior of the API across both REST and GraphQL.

item.name: Get Aggregated Data without Token (REST)
item.description:
Calls the protected REST aggregation endpoint without an Authorization header. Expected to return 401 Unauthorized since the controller is decorated with [Authorize].

Endpoint: `GET /api/aggregation`

item.name: Get Aggregated Data with Invalid Sort Field (REST)
item.description:
Calls the REST aggregation endpoint with an invalid `sortBy` value. Expected to return a 400 Bad Request with error code INVALID_SORT_FIELD, handled by ExceptionHandlingMiddleware.

Endpoint: `GET /api/aggregation`

exec: pm.test("Status code is 400 Bad Request", function () {
pm.test("Response contains INVALID_SORT_FIELD error code", function () {

item.name: Aggregate Data without Token (GraphQL)
item.description:
Calls the protected `aggregateData` field without an Authorization header. Expected to return a GraphQL authorization error since the field is decorated with [Authorize].

GraphQL field: `aggregateData`

exec: pm.test("Response contains an authorization error", function () {

item.name: Aggregate Data with Invalid Sort Field (GraphQL)
item.description:
Calls `aggregateData` with an invalid `sortBy` value. Expected to return a GraphQL error with code INVALID_SORT_FIELD, handled by InvalidSortFieldExceptionFilter.

GraphQL field: `aggregateData`

exec: pm.test("Response contains an INVALID_SORT_FIELD error", function () {

item.name: Authenticate User with Invalid Credentials
item.description:
Calls the `login` mutation with incorrect credentials. Expected to return `success: false` and a null token.

GraphQL field: `login`
MediatR command: `LoginCommand` / `LoginCommandHandler`

exec: pm.test("Login fails for invalid credentials", function () {
